task.name: Parallel /tmux:execute supervisor
task.objective: Spawn N parallel /tmux:execute workers via tmux-cli with collision-safe window names. Workers save a full .md report and reply with a filepath-only DONE tag; supervisor Reads the file, verifies it against the pushback catalogue, pushes back up to 3x on gaps, and kills windows on acceptance.

input.arg: Path to a task file OR inline task description. Task-file format is free-form; the supervisor reads it, derives fan-out shape per <ranking>, and proceeds.
Optional domain hint for the ranking heuristic (e.g. "security-research", "codebase-audit", "docs-pass"). Defaults to the research-root's inferred domain.

output.primary: Inline user-facing synthesis text: accepted findings merged, overlaps/conflicts stated explicitly, queue-change log included (per step 8a).
output.secondary: Session-synthesis .md written to research-root (name: `NN_session-<topic>.md`), citing each per-worker file.

requirements.directory: research-root is resolved in step 0b (relative to project working directory; create if absent): • Standalone (no active goal): .tmux-cli/research/{y-m-d-hh}/ — {y-m-d-hh} expands to current timestamp, e.g. 2026-05-11-20. • Goal mode (GOAL_ID resolved window-name-first in step 0b: supervisor-{ns} → goal-{ns}; the .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal marker only for the bare `supervisor` window): .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/ — NO timestamp subfolder.
requirements.constraint: The fan-out tasks.yaml path is resolved once in step 0b as `tasks-path`: goal-scoped (.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml) in GOAL_MODE, else the top-level .tmux-cli/tasks.yaml. In goal mode the taskvisor daemon reads the per-goal file (retry: exists/reset/inject). This is DISTINCT from plan.xml's top-level planning-queue (the /tmux:plan handoff), which is never moved. Only per-worker report and research-task context .md files live under research-root; tasks-path holds the fan-out and its context: fields point at the (possibly goal-scoped) files.

glossary.term: Name of the supervisor's tmux window (e.g. "supervisor"). Stable for whole session.
Name of a worker's tmux window (e.g. "execute-7"). Each worker gets unique N.

Primary directory where per-worker .md reports and research-task context files are saved. Resolved in step 0b: .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/ when a goal is active (no timestamp), else .tmux-cli/research/{y-m-d-hh}/ (timestamped). Defined in <requirements> / step 0b.

Internal flag set true in step 0b when the supervisor's OWN window name is namespaced (supervisor-{ns}, daemon dispatch at MaxGoals>1) or, for the bare `supervisor` window, when .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal exists and is non-empty. In goal mode research-root is goal-scoped (.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/) and step 9b appends a research-file index to the goal's goal.md.

The goal identifier resolved in step 0b when GOAL_MODE is true: derived from the supervisor's namespaced window name (supervisor-{ns} → goal-{ns}, mirroring parseGoalBinding in internal/mcp/tools.go), else read (trimmed) from .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal for the bare `supervisor` window. Names the goal folder under .tmux-cli/goals/.

Path to the supervisor's fan-out tasks.yaml, resolved in step 0b: .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml in GOAL_MODE (read by the taskvisor daemon on retry), else the top-level .tmux-cli/tasks.yaml. Every fan-out read/write/archive/status-flip in this flow uses tasks-path. DISTINCT from plan.xml's top-level planning-queue.
One-line human-readable description of what a worker is doing (e.g. "implement setup config parser").
Supervisor's window name in tag messages.
Worker's window name in tag messages.
Priority ordering used for auto fan-out; see <ranking> inside step 3.
Supervisor operates without user confirmation after initial invocation; see <autonomy-policy>.
The G1–G5 taxonomy in step 6 used to gate DONE acceptance.
Internal flag set when tasks.yaml was created by /tmux:plan (detected in step 1c). Causes steps 3 and 3b to skip fan-out planning.

Counter of self-generated waves: a wave whose tasks originate from the supervisor's OWN synthesis/step-8a output rather than from requirements traceable to the original $ARGUMENTS input. Each such wave increments SELF_WAVE; the cap is 2 per CHAIN (see <stop-conditions>). Waves whose tasks map to the original input's requirements do NOT increment SELF_WAVE and are never capped. STARTING VALUE: 0 for a fresh human-authorized invocation — but NOT unconditionally. When $ARGUMENTS is a .md plan file whose frontmatter carries `self_wave` (a fresh-context handoff written by step 9b), step 0c ADOPTS that value as the starting counter. A "chain" is the whole sequence of contexts linked by fresh-handoff restarts, so the cap survives every /clear; zeroing SELF_WAVE at the top of an adopted invocation would launder the cap and is the one thing this counter exists to prevent.

Per-goal execution lane, decided UPSTREAM (at /tmux:task-list consume or /tmux:plan step 3d) and only READ by this supervisor. Resolution order: tasks-path top-level `lane` key → goal.md `## Lane` section → full. Exactly the value `solo` (the solo-lane) routes a single-pending-task goal through step 3c's inline execution; `full`, an absent lane, or any malformed/unknown value resolves to full (the worker path). The supervisor NEVER writes, decides, or infers the lane.

message-tags.note: All supervisor↔worker communication is tagged. Use tags verbatim; semantics below are the source of truth.
message-tags.tag: Worker reports completion. Body carries NO inline findings — the .md at file= IS the report.
Worker cannot proceed without a fact. Reason ≤ 200 chars inline. Supervisor answers via windows-message to that wid, or replies [SUPERVISOR:NARROW-SCOPE].
Worker hit a terminal failure. Reason ≤ 200 chars inline. Supervisor respawns with narrowed scope (first attempt) or escalates (second).

Worker is blocked by a prerequisite that is OUT of this goal's scope (a separate goal must be built first). Distinct from FAILED (in-scope blocker → implementer retry) and NEED_INPUT (missing fact). Supervisor records a goal-scoped escalation marker (escalation.md), suppresses the code-retry, and lets the validator relay it to generation — it does NOT respawn the same scope. Routed per step 5's ESCALATE branch (cap 2/goal). need= MUST be a concrete buildable prerequisite; contentless need= is REMINDed, not relayed.
Supervisor reminds worker of protocol (e.g. file-only DONE). Does not advance state.
Supervisor requests a DELIVERABLE fix per pushback catalogue. Worker updates the SAME .md file and re-tags DONE.
Supervisor amends a running worker's scope mid-run. Worker applies to current task.
Supervisor amends the response protocol mid-run (e.g. new save-path rule).
Supervisor instructs worker to exit cleanly because its scope is now pointless (per step 5a). Worker ACKs then exits.
Supervisor narrows a NEED_INPUT-blocked worker's scope instead of escalating to user.

Supervisor resolved a SMALL out-of-scope prerequisite IN-RUN (a narrow prereq-* worker built and committed it) and RESUMES the ESCALATE-blocked worker instead of bouncing the whole goal to generation. Carries the resolving commit <sha>. UNCHARGED — no code-retry decrement, no SpecRetry. The worker re-checks the blocker and continues; if it is still blocked it replies [EXECUTE:FAILED reason=prereq-ineffective]. This is the SOLE accepted continuation of an [EXECUTE:ESCALATE]; goal-sized needs and in-run failures fall back to the generation relay verbatim.

escalation-routing.note: How an [EXECUTE:ESCALATE ...] from a worker becomes a re-planning bounce. Source of truth for step 5's ESCALATE branch.
escalation-routing.why: The supervisor cannot re-plan directly: a SupervisorSignal carries only {source,status,timestamp} and its status is stored but never routed to generation; the SOLE route to bounceToGeneration is a validator's ValidatorSignal with verdict=blocked + owner=planner. So the supervisor records a goal-scoped marker and lets the next validation cycle relay it. The supervisor also must NOT call goal-create (it races the daemon on goals.yaml) — generation owns that.
escalation-routing.marker-file: .tmux-cli/goals/<GOAL_ID>/escalation.md — supervisor-written, validator-read (validate.xml/investigate.xml), generation-read. Idempotent across cycles: increment escalation_count, append entries; generation supersedes/clears consumed entries when it creates the prerequisite goal.
escalation-routing.schema: ## Escalations escalation_count: <N> - need: "<one-line buildable prerequisite>" from: execute-<N> cycle: <C> status: in-run-attempt resolved: in-run (cycle <C>, commit <sha>)
escalation-routing.schema-note: The `status:` and `resolved:` lines are OPTIONAL same-block continuations of their `- need:` entry — written AFTER the `- need:`/`from:` lines and BEFORE the next `- need:` entry or EOF, byte-for-byte what the daemon's parseEscalationMd reads. They are mutually exclusive and transient: `status: in-run-attempt` is present only WHILE a prereq-* worker attempts the need in-run; on success it is REWRITTEN to `resolved: in-run (cycle <C>, commit <sha>)`; on prereq failure it is STRIPPED, leaving a bare `- need:` entry. A goal-sized need that relays straight to generation carries NEITHER continuation line.
escalation-routing.resolved-note: An entry carrying a `resolved:` line was handled IN-RUN and is NOT relayed to generation — validate.xml / investigate.xml skip it; only entries lacking a `resolved:` line become blocked+planner spec-defect findings. Every entry (in-run OR relayed) still counts toward the <cap> below.
escalation-routing.cap: Cap escalations at 2 per goal (≤ MaxSpecRetries). This is a supervisor-side guard against many escalations within one implementation cycle; the daemon's bounceToGeneration hard-halt at SpecRetries=0 is the authoritative ceiling. Beyond the cap the supervisor stops relaying and the goal takes its normal terminal path (no infinite re-plan loop).
escalation-routing.relayed-finding: The validator MUST emit the relayed finding as status=blocked, owner=planner, failure_class=spec-defect with a NON-CONTENTLESS detail+correction (e.g. correction="Create prerequisite goal: <need>"). HasSubstantiveSpecDefect rejects a finding whose detail AND correction are both contentless — that would mis-route to ValidationRetries instead of generation. That is why step 5 rejects a contentless need= up front.

autonomy-policy.rule: Supervisor operates AUTONOMOUSLY after the initial /supervisor invocation. The initial delegation authorizes the entire chain until a <stop-conditions> rule fires.
Do NOT ask the user to: pick between options, sequence workers, approve continuation, confirm a findings-driven replan, amend a running worker, or spawn a new follow-up.
For ambiguity: pick the highest-yield interpretation per <ranking> and record the chosen rationale in the step-9 synthesis so the user can redirect.
Escalate to user ONLY at one of the two defined exits: (a) zero executable workers can be constructed from input, OR (b) a <stop-conditions> rule fires.

Decision-resolution policy (quality-in-scope): a worker decision/verdict/correction presenting RESOLVABLE options (a technical/architectural fork, a waiver-vs-quality choice) is auto-resolved — answer the worker with the highest-quality option that does NOT expand the goal's scope (real domain types over asserted strings, no waivers, no shortcuts, no new tech-debt), plus a one-line rationale. Consistent with the user-only-knowledge escalation below: bounce to the user ONLY for credentials, intent, or out-of-band facts no file-reading can supply; a purely technical/architectural choice is NEVER user-only.

Synthesis output (§9) is NOT a stop gate — writing a session-synthesis .md is routine. After writing it, re-run <step n="8a"> replan against its own contents; any "proposed next wave" / "queue changes" / "follow-up workers" / "wave N+1" sections in the synthesis are executable-queue entries, spawn them per §3 fan-out (subject to the SELF_WAVE cap in <stop-conditions> — purely self-generated waves are capped at 2 per invocation).

Self-imposed stage gates (plan documents saying "Stage 2 after Stage 1", "wave 6 after wave 5", "deferred to next session", numbered-wave boundaries) are ORGANIZATIONAL, not authorizational. The initial /supervisor invocation authorizes the entire chain until a real <stop-conditions> fires. Do NOT treat a stage gate you wrote yourself as a user-imposed pause.

llm.mandate: NEVER bypass the supervisor protocol. The user invoked /tmux:supervisor deliberately — follow the full flow (steps 0→9) regardless of perceived task simplicity. "Too simple for workers" is NOT a valid reason to skip. Even a single-worker linear task goes through fan-out, tasks.yaml, spawn, poll, accept, synthesize. SOLE solo-lane carve-out (data-driven only): when the upstream-decided lane resolves to solo (resolution order: tasks-path top-level `lane` key → goal.md `## Lane` → full) AND exactly 1 task is pending in tasks-path, step 3c executes that task inline in this window with zero spawns — perceived simplicity remains an INVALID activation reason; only the upstream lane value activates this carve-out, never the supervisor's own judgment.
Execute steps ONE BY ONE in EXACT ORDER
Create TODO list for tracking spawned workers (SELF_WID + subtask label)
Operate per <autonomy-policy> — do not ask the user outside the two defined exits.
NEVER spawn a worker before tasks.yaml is written and verified (step 3b gate)
NEVER call /worker:execute — use /tmux:execute which is the correct skill name
NEVER use sleep — idle is the trigger for incoming windows-message
windows-kill takes the window NAME (e.g. "execute-3"), not an @id. windows-send accepts @N; kill does not.
When PRE_PLANNED is true, do NOT re-derive fan-out — trust the plan. Only verify context files exist (step 3b) and spawn (step 4).

Resolve research-root AND tasks-path in step 0b before any worker spawn — every supervisor-created context file and worker report save path uses research-root (goal-scoped in GOAL_MODE, timestamped otherwise); every fan-out tasks.yaml read/write/archive/status-flip uses tasks-path (goal-scoped in GOAL_MODE, else top-level). PRE_PLANNED context: paths from /tmux:plan are NOT overridden.

step.title: Clean slate
step.action: Remove .tmux-cli/audit-done guard file if it exists (allows the unplanned-audit hook to fire at the end of this cycle).

Remove .tmux-cli/cycle-restart-queued sentinel if it exists (per-Stop serialization marker owned by the Stop hooks; the cycle hook touches it before queuing this relaunch and the unplanned-audit hook consumes it to yield — clearing it here re-arms the next cycle's unplanned-audit).
Remove .tmux-cli/auto-execute-guard if it exists (consumed by the Stop hook to prevent double-invocation after plan auto-execute).

Remove a STALE .tmux-cli/fresh-handoff marker if it exists, and LOG it: "Stale fresh-handoff marker removed (plan=<its plan: value>) — the Stop hook never consumed it." The marker is one-shot and is consumed (rm) by the tmux-supervisor-cycle.sh Stop hook BEFORE it sends anything, so a marker that survives to a fresh supervisor boot means the hook never fired (missing/failed hook). Deleting it here prevents it ambushing a later, unrelated Stop with a restart onto a stale plan. Do this BEFORE step 0c reads $ARGUMENTS frontmatter — the marker is never the counter source; the plan file is.

Resolve the fan-out file path the same way step 0b will (GOAL_MODE → .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml with GOAL_ID derived window-name-first per step 0b — supervisor-{ns} → goal-{ns}; the .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal marker only for the bare `supervisor` window — else .tmux-cli/tasks.yaml) — this is tasks-path. If tasks-path exists and is NOT a continuation cycle, set its top-level status field to "planning" — EXCEPTION: when $ARGUMENTS leads with a goal-{ns} token (daemon dispatchRetry or /tmux:plan auto-execute handoff), do NOT flip the status. The per-goal tasks-path was finalized (status: ready) by the planner or reset by the daemon's resetTaskStatuses precisely so step 1c consumes it as PRE_PLANNED — flipping it to "planning" would mask the handoff and re-plan an already-planned goal. If it doesn't exist yet, the status will be set when tasks-path is first created in step 3.

step.title: Resolve research-root
step.action: Read the FIRST whitespace-delimited token of $ARGUMENTS. If it matches ^goal-\d+$ it is an AUTHORITATIVE goal id handed over by the taskvisor daemon (dispatchRetry sends `/tmux:supervisor goal-{ns}`) or the /tmux:plan auto-execute handoff. Call it ARG_GOAL_ID (empty when no such leading token is present).

branch.condition: ARG_GOAL_ID is set (leading $ARGUMENTS token matches goal-{ns})
branch.action: GOAL mode (argument binding — HIGHEST PRECEDENCE for GOAL_ID, above both window-name derivation and the global marker). Set GOAL_ID = ARG_GOAL_ID. This is the AUTHORITATIVE goal id: the daemon shipped it explicitly BECAUSE the global .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal marker is last-writer-wins under concurrent dispatch and may name ANOTHER goal — do NOT read that marker and do NOT re-derive GOAL_ID from the window name. Set research-root = .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/ (NO timestamp). Set tasks-path = .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml (the per-goal fan-out file the daemon reads on retry). Create the goal dir / research-root if absent. Set internal flag GOAL_MODE=true.

Determine SUPERVISOR_WID by reading the per-goal marker .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/supervisor-window and using its content VERBATIM (trim only the trailing newline/whitespace) as the window name. Do NOT probe your own tmux window name and do NOT re-derive the name from the goal id. The daemon wrote this marker byte-exact (via supervisorWindow(goal.ID, MaxGoals)) BEFORE booting this window: a daemon-dispatched goal supervisor is ALWAYS the namespaced supervisor-{ns} (at every MaxGoals, including 1), which parseGoalBinding maps back to {GOAL_ID}, so worker routing and GOAL_ID agree. The bare `supervisor` denotes ONLY window-0 / a standalone interactive supervisor and is never a goal window — reading the marker verbatim therefore binds SUPERVISOR_WID to the exact spawned window (a synthesized name or window-name probe could miss it). This replaces the old active-window self-identification probe, whose only mechanism (a tmux window-name query via a foreign Bash shell) returned the session's active window rather than this agent's pane. Pass this exact SUPERVISOR_WID as the supervisorWid argument to EVERY windows-spawn-worker call so the worker prefix, report path, and reply target bind correctly even when the MCP server's own resolveSelfWindowName returns empty. Log: "Goal mode (argument binding): GOAL_ID={GOAL_ID}, SUPERVISOR_WID=<marker content>, research-root=.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/, tasks-path=.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml".

branch.condition: ARG_GOAL_ID is NOT set (no leading goal-{ns} token in $ARGUMENTS — interactive /tmux:supervisor, or a plain task-file/inline arg)
branch.action: Do NOT probe your own tmux window name to self-identify. The old window-name binding (deriving GOAL_ID = goal-{ns} from a supervisor-{ns} SELF_NAME, mirroring parseGoalBinding in internal/mcp/tools.go) relied on an active-window probe, but its only mechanism returned the session's ACTIVE window rather than this agent's pane — unreliable at MaxGoals>1 — so window-name self-identification is dropped. With no arg, GOAL_ID precedence collapses to the global marker only.
Check whether .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal exists and is non-empty.

GOAL mode. Read GOAL_ID from the file (trim whitespace). Set research-root = .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/ (NO timestamp). Set tasks-path = .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml (the per-goal fan-out file the daemon reads on retry). Create the goal dir / research-root if absent. Set internal flag GOAL_MODE=true. Determine SUPERVISOR_WID by reading .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/supervisor-window VERBATIM (trim only the trailing newline/whitespace) — the daemon wrote that per-goal marker byte-exact and a goal supervisor is ALWAYS the namespaced supervisor-{ns} (at every MaxGoals, including 1; parseGoalBinding maps it back to goal-{ns}, so MCP research-root routing and this GOAL_ID agree). Bare `supervisor` is window-0/standalone only, never a goal window. Because the global .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal marker is last-writer-wins under concurrent dispatch and may name ANOTHER goal, the daemon prefers shipping GOAL_ID as a leading arg (the arg branch above); this marker path is the interactive fallback. Pass SUPERVISOR_WID as the supervisorWid argument to EVERY windows-spawn-worker call. Log: "Goal mode: GOAL_ID={GOAL_ID}, SUPERVISOR_WID=<marker content>, research-root=.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/, tasks-path=.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml".

STANDALONE mode. Set research-root = .tmux-cli/research/{y-m-d-hh}/ ({y-m-d-hh} = current timestamp). Set tasks-path = .tmux-cli/tasks.yaml. Create research-root if absent. Set internal flag GOAL_MODE=false. Set SUPERVISOR_WID = bare `supervisor`. Log: "Standalone mode: research-root=.tmux-cli/research/{y-m-d-hh}/, tasks-path=.tmux-cli/tasks.yaml, SUPERVISOR_WID=supervisor".

step.rule: Every later reference to research-root AND tasks-path in this flow uses the values resolved here. tasks-path is the fan-out file — goal-scoped in GOAL_MODE, top-level otherwise — and is DISTINCT from plan.xml's top-level planning-queue (which is never moved).

PRE_PLANNED tasks already carry resolved context: paths written by /tmux:plan — step 0b does NOT override those. It governs only the paths the supervisor itself creates: its own fan-out context files (step 3) and worker report save paths (steps 5/task-message).

Step 0b resolves PATHS only. When $ARGUMENTS is a .md plan file handed over by a fresh-context restart, its frontmatter ALSO carries the SELF_WAVE/cycle counters — those are adopted in step 0c, not here.

step.title: Adopt handoff counters from a plan-file frontmatter
step.action: Determine whether $ARGUMENTS (after any leading goal-{ns} token consumed by step 0b) names a single existing .md plan file. If it does not (inline free-form text, a tasks.yaml path, or a missing file), SKIP this step: SELF_WAVE starts at 0 and the cycle counter comes from tasks-path as usual.
Read the plan file's leading YAML frontmatter block (the `---` fenced block at the very top of the file, if present) and look for the keys `self_wave` and `cycle`.

check.condition: frontmatter carries self_wave and/or cycle
check.action: ADOPT them as this invocation's STARTING counters instead of 0: set SELF_WAVE = the frontmatter `self_wave` value (when present), and seed the cycle counter from the frontmatter `cycle` value (when present) so step 9b's increment and the max_cycles comparison count from the right place. Log: "Adopted handoff counters from <plan-file>: SELF_WAVE=<n>, cycle=<m>."
check.rule: This is the cap-integrity half of the fresh-handoff contract. A self-generated wave handed off via /tmux:supervisor:fresh restarts this window with a CLEARED context, so without adoption every /clear would reset SELF_WAVE to 0 and turn the handoff into a cap-laundering loop. Adoption makes the <stop-conditions> cap of 2 self-generated waves hold across the whole chain, not merely per-context.

check.condition: no frontmatter, or neither key present
check.action: Start both counters as usual (SELF_WAVE = 0; cycle from tasks-path). A hand-typed `/tmux:supervisor plan.md` — and a manual `/tmux:supervisor:fresh plan.md`, which deliberately omits the counters — is a NEW human authorization, so caps legitimately reset.

step.title: MCP precondition
step.action: Call windows-list

check.condition: tmux-cli not available
check.action: Print exactly: ERROR: tmux-cli MCP required but not available — enable it and retry.
STOP. Do not create any windows.

step.title: Read project context
step.action: Check if AGENTS.md exists in the project root. If it does, Read it and internalize the project conventions, architecture, and constraints before proceeding.
If AGENTS.md does not exist, check for CLAUDE.md instead. Read whichever is found first.
If neither exists, proceed without project context.
step.rule: This context MUST inform your fan-out decisions, task scoping, and worker instructions throughout the session.

step.title: Tasks gate
step.note: This gate operates on tasks-path (resolved in step 0b): the per-goal fan-out file in GOAL_MODE, the top-level planning-queue in standalone. PRE_PLANNED handoffs from /tmux:plan are detected on tasks-path in BOTH modes — plan.xml resolves the same tasks-path (per-goal in GOAL_MODE, top-level in standalone) and finalizes the queue there.
step.action: Check if tasks-path exists in the project working directory.

check.condition: tasks-path exists AND top-level status=ready AND has tasks with status=pending (pre-planned by /tmux:plan)
all pending tasks have a context field pointing to an existing .md file
check.action: PRE-PLANNED session detected (created by /tmux:plan). Log: "Pre-planned tasks detected (N pending). Consuming plan."
Set internal flag: PRE_PLANNED=true.
Proceed to step 2.

check.condition: some pending tasks are missing context files
check.action: STOP. Print: "Pre-planned tasks found but N context files are missing: [list missing paths]. Run /tmux:plan to fix, or archive and start fresh."

check.condition: tasks-path exists AND has tasks with status=pending or status=in_progress
this supervisor was called WITH tasks-path as $ARGUMENTS (continuation cycle)
check.action: IN_PROGRESS RECOVERY (mirrors plan.xml step 0c): collect every task with status=in_progress from tasks-path. If any exist, call windows-list ONCE — an in_progress task whose window is dead (name absent from windows-list, or window-id mismatch per step 4's worker-windows.yaml LIVENESS RULE) is an ORPHAN whose worker died with the previous cycle: reset it to status=pending in tasks-path and log "Recovered {wid} (in_progress, dead window) → pending — step 4 respawns it". If the windows-list call fails, reset NOTHING (fail-safe — liveness cannot be verified).
This IS a continuation cycle — proceed to step 2 and work on pending tasks from tasks-path.

check.condition: this supervisor was called with OTHER input (new invocation) AND GOAL_MODE is true
check.action: Apply the same IN_PROGRESS RECOVERY as the continuation branch above (reset in_progress tasks with dead windows to pending; windows-list failure → reset nothing).

Proceed WITHOUT user interaction: treat the pending/in_progress tasks in tasks-path as the work queue and continue to step 2. The per-goal queue is owned by the daemon/planner handoff — unfinished tasks here mean "execute these", and a daemon-dispatched supervisor has no human to answer a stop gate (the silence would burn the goal's cycle timeout as a misclassified code defect).

check.condition: this supervisor was called with OTHER input (new invocation) AND GOAL_MODE is false
check.action: STOP. Print to user: "STOP GATE: Unfinished tasks found in {tasks-path}. [list each task name and status]. To continue previous work, re-run without new input. To start fresh, reply 'archive and start new'."
If user says "archive and start new": move tasks-path to .tmux-cli/tasks/{y-m-d-hh}/tasks-{mm}.yaml (create directory if needed) and proceed with a fresh tasks-path.
Wait for user response before proceeding.

check.condition: tasks-path does not exist
check.action: Proceed normally to step 2.

check.condition: tasks-path exists AND all tasks have status=done
check.action: Archive the completed tasks-path to .tmux-cli/tasks/{y-m-d-hh}/tasks-{mm}.yaml (create directory if needed), then remove the original tasks-path file.
Proceed normally to step 2 with a clean slate.

step.title: Spec-repair intake
step.note: plan-once (backend task 490): once a goal has run its first /tmux:plan, the daemon downgrades any later DispatchPlan (spec-defect bounce or escalation route) to DispatchSpecRepair, which dispatches THIS supervisor (/tmux:supervisor {GOAL_ID}) instead of re-running the full planner. When that happens, the SPEC DEFECT correction the planner would have consumed is already on disk under the goal dir — this step amends the EXISTING tasks-path in place rather than re-planning from scratch. A pure escalation route (no SPEC DEFECT correction) falls through unchanged to the normal supervisor flow, which already resolves micro-prerequisites in-run.
step.action: Locate the highest-numbered .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/corrections/cycle-N.md (largest N). If the corrections directory is absent or empty, set SPEC_REPAIR=false and proceed to step 2 unchanged.

Read that cycle-N.md. If its content LEADS WITH the SPEC DEFECT framing header (it begins with the literal text "Validation reports a SPEC DEFECT (owner: PLANNER)"), set SPEC_REPAIR=true. Otherwise set SPEC_REPAIR=false and proceed to step 2 unchanged.

check.condition: SPEC_REPAIR is true
check.action: Amend tasks-path (the per-goal .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml resolved in step 0b) IN PLACE — do NOT re-plan or regenerate the whole fan-out. From the correction detail (the framing header plus the rendered per-finding blocks below it): fix ONLY the defective task's scope / acceptance / validate fields, and add a single micro prerequisite task entry ONLY if the correction explicitly demands one (with its own context .md file per step 3 task-tracking).

Set status to "pending" for ONLY the affected task(s); leave every already-done task at status: done. Keep the top-level status at "planning" until step 3b re-validates, then continue to step 2 → 3b → 4 to dispatch ONLY the amended task(s).
check.rule: MaxSpecRetries and the spec-convergence circuit-breaker (daemon-owned) remain the unchanged bounds — this step never edits goals.yaml, retry counters, or the correction file; it only amends tasks-path and re-runs the affected task(s).

step.action: If SPEC_REPAIR is false, proceed to step 2 with no change (PRE_PLANNED implement / in-run prerequisite resolution paths are untouched).

step.title: Identify SUPERVISOR_WID — verify the step-0b binding
step.rule: SUPERVISOR_WID is ALREADY BOUND: step 0b resolved it authoritatively in ALL branches (per-goal supervisor-window marker read verbatim in GOAL_MODE; bare `supervisor` in standalone). Do NOT re-derive it here and do NOT overwrite it — step 0b documents that the active-window probe is unreliable at MaxGoals>1 (it names the session's ACTIVE window, not this agent's pane).
step.action: VERIFY only: confirm a window whose name equals the step-0b SUPERVISOR_WID appears in the step-1 windows-list output. If it is ABSENT, log the discrepancy ("SUPERVISOR_WID={name} not found in windows-list — keeping the step-0b binding") but KEEP the step-0b value: the daemon-written marker is authoritative, and the window may simply not be the active one.

branch.condition: step 0b somehow left SUPERVISOR_WID unset (should not happen)
branch.fallback: Last resort only: from windows-list, pick the entry with active: true if present. If the tmux-cli implementation does not expose active:true (e.g. returns only {"name":...}), and exactly one window exists, treat its NAME as SUPERVISOR_WID. If multiple windows exist and none is marked active: if a window named "supervisor" exists in windows-list, that is your SUPERVISOR_WID — use it without asking.

step.action: Store SUPERVISOR_WID (name form, e.g. "supervisor") for the whole session.

step.title: Fan-out decision

check.condition: PRE_PLANNED is true
check.action: Skip fan-out planning — tasks were pre-planned by /tmux:plan.
Read tasks-path for the task list. Each task entry has: name, wid, status, context (path to .md file).
Proceed directly to step 3b for verification.

step.rule: 1 worker when task is linear OR subtasks share MUTABLE state (same SHM slot, same DB row, same file being written)
N workers when subtasks are file-disjoint OR topic-disjoint AND mutually independent

Default to MAXIMUM parallelism. Serial-by-default is a failure mode. Sharing a read-only binary, a codebase, or a common ancestor finding is NOT shared state — only shared MUTABLE state blocks parallelism. Before picking serial, ask: "Can I write a complete self-contained task-message for the dependent worker right now?" If yes → PARALLEL. Only serialize when a later worker's SCOPE is literally unconstructable without the prior worker's output (e.g., the exact VA to audit is unknown until the prior worker discovers it), and record the missing fact that justifies the serialization.
If a subtask depends on another's output, spawn the prerequisite first, accept its DONE, then spawn the dependent — don't pretend they're parallel.

The 0-worker solo-lane route (step 3c) is DATA-DRIVEN ONLY: it activates exclusively on the upstream-decided lane value resolved in step 3c, never on the fan-out reasoning in this step and never on perceived task simplicity — the supervisor never sets the lane. The shape rules above govern only goals that fall through step 3c to the full worker path.
step.example: "compare caching in A vs B" → 2 workers, one per module
"audit every worker/*.md for bug X" → N workers, one per file
"TODO with 6 disjoint P1 items in same binary" → 6 parallel workers (shared binary ≠ shared state)
step.ambiguity: When the task is ambiguous (multiple valid fan-out shapes, unclear priority across TODO items, "do the follow-ups" input), apply <autonomy-policy> — pick per <ranking> and proceed.
step.ranking: Highest-yield-first for auto-fan-out: (1) closest to user-facing surface or critical path, (2) closest to impact target, (3) smallest budget before completion, (4) overturns prior assumption if confirmed. Break ties by lowest budget. If two options are budget-tied and yield-tied, pick the one that runs first in dependency order (prerequisite before dependent).
step.exhaustive-coverage: NEVER skip a possible path due to low probability of success. Every identified vector — even speculative or low-yield — must be executed to produce a precise ruling (confirmed, refuted with evidence, or blocked by specific barrier). "Not worth budget" and "too speculative" are NOT valid reasons to skip a worker. The goal is exhaustive coverage: every path precisely documented as viable or dead, with no gaps left to assumption.

task-tracking.action: After determining the fan-out shape, create or update tasks-path (resolved in step 0b) with all planned tasks. Each task entry has: name (subtask label), wid (execute-N), status (pending), context (path to .md file), and cycle (current cycle number, starting at 1).

Before writing a task to tasks-path, create a context .md file for it in research-root (resolved in step 0b) using the slug pattern {research-root}/task-<slug>.md. The context field MUST be set on every task — it is mandatory.
If tasks-path already exists from a continuation cycle, preserve done tasks and update/add pending ones.
task-tracking.format: tasks-path structure: status: planning, cycle: N, tasks: [{name: "...", wid: "execute-N", status: pending, context: "{research-root}/task-<slug>.md"}]
task-tracking.rule: The literal value written for context: is the RESOLVED research-root path (step 0b) — goal-scoped (.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/research/task-<slug>.md) in goal mode, timestamped (.tmux-cli/research/{y-m-d-hh}/task-<slug>.md) in standalone mode. The fan-out file itself is tasks-path (goal-scoped in GOAL_MODE, top-level otherwise).

When first creating or updating tasks-path during fan-out planning, set the top-level status to "planning". This prevents Stop hooks (cycle restart, unplanned audit) from firing while the supervisor is still planning.

step.title: Tasks gate — verify tasks-path before spawn
step.action: Read tasks-path and verify it contains ALL planned tasks with status=pending.

check.condition: tasks-path does not exist OR is missing planned tasks
check.action: STOP. Do NOT proceed to step 4. Write tasks-path first per step 3 task-tracking, then re-verify.

step.action: Call the tasks-validate MCP tool. In GOAL_MODE pass goal_id={GOAL_ID} so it validates the per-goal file (.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/tasks.yaml); in standalone mode call it with no goal_id so it validates the top-level planning-queue. If valid=false, fix every reported error (remove extra fields, move content to context .md file) and re-run until valid=true.

check.condition: tasks-path exists AND contains all planned pending tasks AND tasks-validate returns valid=true
check.action: Set the top-level status in tasks-path to "ready". This arms the Stop hooks (cycle restart, unplanned audit) so they can fire once workers finish.
check.note: OVERLOADED STATUS — top-level `status: ready` carries TWO meanings across flows: in plan.xml it means "finalized plan awaiting supervisor consumption"; HERE it is set BEFORE spawning and means "fan-out armed for Stop hooks". Step 1c's PRE_PLANNED detection distinguishes the two by the SHAPE of the context files (plan-written spec .md files vs supervisor-written research-task files). Doc-only clarification — no behavior change.
check.action: Proceed to step 3c (solo-lane inline gate — non-solo goals fall through to step 4 unchanged).

step.rule: This is a BLOCKING GATE. No worker may be spawned (step 4) until tasks-path is written, verified, tasks-validate passes, AND its status is set to "ready".

step.title: Solo-lane inline execution
step.condition: lane resolution (tasks-path top-level `lane` key → goal.md `## Lane` → full) == solo AND exactly 1 pending task
step.note: The solo-lane carve-out collapses a 1-task goal's worker boot: the supervisor implements the single task inline in THIS window instead of spawning an execute-N worker, then converges on the untouched steps 9/9b. The lane is decided UPSTREAM (task-list consume / plan step 3d) and only READ here — this step never evaluates lane-gate criteria and never self-declares solo.
step.action: Resolve the lane (read-only): (1) if tasks-path carries a top-level `lane` key, its trimmed value IS the lane — presence wins precedence, goal.md is not consulted; (2) else, in GOAL_MODE, if goal.md has a `## Lane` section, its bare trimmed body is the lane; (3) else the lane is `full`. Map the value: exactly `solo` → solo; `full`, empty, or ANY unknown/malformed value (e.g. `banana`) → full — log the malformed value and proceed full. NEVER read goals.yaml for the lane.
Count tasks with status=pending in tasks-path.

check.condition: lane != solo OR pending count != 1
check.action: Fall through to step 4 UNCHANGED — the full worker path. This is the default; for every lane-absent goal the flow is byte-identical to before this step existed.

check.condition: lane == solo AND exactly 1 pending task
check.action: Log: "solo-lane route: lane=solo, 1 pending task — implementing inline in {SUPERVISOR_WID}, zero spawns."

Read the task's context .md (its context: field). Implement the task IN THIS WINDOW under /tmux:execute step-3 semantics: stay strictly within the task's SCOPE (adjacent issues go under RISKS, not extra work), auto-resolve decidable forks per the Decision-resolution policy (quality-in-scope, rationale recorded in the report), and wrap any shared-DB schema mutation in `flock .tmux-cli/db.lock -c '<cmd>'`. At MaxGoals>1 this window is already cwd'd into the goal's worktree, so inline edits and validate runs land there with no extra plumbing.

Run EVERY command listed under goal.md's `## Validation Rules` via Bash, each wrapped as `timeout 120s <command>`. On an in-scope failure: fix it and re-run the failing command. If a command is STILL red after in-scope fixes, record the failure honestly in the report and proceed — the daemon's native validate re-run is the terminal judge; do not loop forever, do not widen scope.
Standalone solo: there is no goal.md, hence no `## Validation Rules` to run — skip local validation and note that in the report.
Self-check the report ONCE against step 6's G1–G5 pushback catalogue before saving (the inline path has no second agent to push back).

Write the report to {research-root}/execute-inline-<slug>.md with the standard ## FINDINGS / ## RISKS / ## RECOMMENDATION / ## FILES sections; under FINDINGS include each validate command, its exit status, and a short output excerpt.
Mark the task status=done in tasks-path (direct pending→done, no in_progress flip — parity with the worker path's bookkeeping, so the task-list reconcile contract is untouched).

SKIP steps 4–7 entirely — no windows-spawn-worker, no poll/accept, no pushback round-trip, no windows-kill, and NO idle waits (the inline work itself keeps the lead pane's heartbeat alive). Continue DIRECTLY at step 9: with zero workers spawned its all-workers-killed condition is trivially satisfied, and steps 9/9b run UNCHANGED — same research-file index, same cycle bookkeeping, same completion-signal write path.

step.rule: The lane is READ, never decided: this supervisor never writes a lane value, never evaluates lane-gate criteria, and never activates this step on its own judgment of task simplicity — only the upstream-decided data activates the solo-lane.

step.title: Spawn protocol (repeat per worker, sequentially within your turn)
step.action: ONCE before the first spawn, resolve WORKTREE_DIR: 1. Check your own current working directory (pwd). If its path contains "/.tmux-cli/worktrees/" → WORKTREE_DIR = that cwd (the daemon -c'd this supervisor window into the goal's worktree at dispatch — authoritative even when the marker below is absent or stale). 2. Else read .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-worktree (trimmed). If present, non-empty, AND its final path component equals GOAL_ID → WORKTREE_DIR = marker content. (The GOAL_ID guard rejects a concurrent goal's stale marker — it is written at validator creation, not at dispatch.) 3. Else leave WORKTREE_DIR unset — this is the MaxGoals=1 / no-worktree case.

Call windows-spawn-worker with: - supervisorWid: your SUPERVISOR_WID name - subtask: one-line label for this worker - contextFile: path to the context .md file created in step 3 - scope: derived from the context .md file (read it and summarize key scope — files, directories, what to investigate or implement) - context: derived from the context .md file (prior findings, constraints, non-goals — if present) - workingDirectory: WORKTREE_DIR if set (resolved above), else OMIT the field. E1: this starts the execute-N worker's shell in the goal's git worktree (tmux -c) so its file edits land in the isolated checkout and merge back. When WORKTREE_DIR is unset, omitting the field keeps the session-default base cwd — byte-identical to before.
The tool atomically: picks the next execute-N name, creates the window, sends /tmux:execute, assembles and sends the task message with DELIVERABLE + RESPONSE PROTOCOL.
Record from the response: workerName, window.tmuxWindowId, taskMessage (for your tracking table).

WID RECONCILE: windows-spawn-worker picks the next free execute-N ITSELF, so under concurrent goals the returned workerName can differ from the wid pre-recorded in tasks-path. If workerName differs from the task's recorded wid, UPDATE the task's wid in tasks-path to workerName AND rewrite any depends_on entries in OTHER tasks that reference the old wid, then log "wid reconciled: <old wid> → <workerName>". Step-5 routing and liveness checks key on wid == actual window name — a stale wid breaks both. In GOAL_MODE the returned workerName is the goal-namespaced `execute-{ns}-N` form (e.g. `execute-003-1`); write it VERBATIM into tasks-path — `tasks-validate` now accepts the namespaced form, so NEVER normalize it back to bare `execute-N`. Step-0c/step-5 liveness and orphan heuristics match on this exact window name.

WINDOW-ID LEDGER: after each spawn, append a row to {research-root}/worker-windows.yaml (create the file if absent): - wid: <workerName> window_id: "<window.tmuxWindowId>" cycle: <current cycle> This is a SIDECAR ledger — NEVER add fields to tasks.yaml task entries (tasks-validate rejects extra fields). Rows are APPEND-ONLY and one wid can carry MULTIPLE rows over a goal's life (the planner and this supervisor share the goal-scoped research-root ledger, and respawns append too) — the MOST RECENT (last-appended) row for a wid is the authoritative one for liveness matching.
step.rule: LIVENESS RULE (name AND id): execute-N names are RECYCLED after kills, so a live window with the right name can belong to ANOTHER goal — making a dead worker look alive. Wherever this flow judges a worker window live/dead by name, when a worker-windows.yaml row exists for that wid, the window counts as THIS task's worker ONLY if BOTH the name AND the tmux window id match the wid's MOST RECENT (last-appended) ledger row — earlier rows for the same wid are stale (a prior spec-worker or pre-respawn window; matching them would misclassify a live current worker as recycled); a name-match with a DIFFERENT id than the most recent row is a RECYCLED name — treat the worker as DEAD. Name-only matching remains the conservative fallback when no ledger row exists.
step.critical: ALWAYS use windows-spawn-worker — never assemble the spawn steps manually via windows-create + windows-send.
step.rule: Report save paths are UNCHANGED by WORKTREE_DIR: research-root and tasks-path stay rooted at the shared .tmux-cli control plane (the worktree symlinks .tmux-cli → base), so worker reports never land inside the worktree's working set.

When consuming pre-planned tasks, read the context .md file for each task and pass to windows-spawn-worker: - subtask: task.name - contextFile: task.context - scope: derived from the context .md file (read it, summarize key scope points) - context: derived from the context .md file (dependencies, constraints, prior findings) - workingDirectory: WORKTREE_DIR if set (resolved above), else OMIT — same rule as the non-pre-planned spawn. After each pre-planned spawn, apply the same WID RECONCILE: if the returned workerName differs from task.wid, update the task's wid (and any depends_on references to the old wid) in tasks-path and log the reconciliation. In GOAL_MODE the returned workerName is the goal-namespaced `execute-{ns}-N` form (e.g. `execute-003-1`); write it VERBATIM into tasks-path — `tasks-validate` now accepts the namespaced form, so NEVER normalize it back to bare `execute-N`. Step-0c/step-5 liveness and orphan heuristics match on this exact window name.

step.title: Idle and route
step.action: Do NOTHING. Wait for windows-message from workers.
step.rule: NEVER use sleep. NEVER poll windows-list while idle.

ORPHAN RECONCILE (event-triggered, NOT polling): on EVERY inbound worker message, BEFORE handling it, if OTHER tasks are in_progress, call windows-list ONCE and reconcile — any in_progress task whose window is dead (name absent from windows-list, or window-id mismatch per step 4's worker-windows.yaml LIVENESS RULE) is an ORPHAN: reset it to status=pending in tasks-path, log "Recovered {wid} (in_progress, dead window) → pending", and respawn it per step 4 AFTER the current message is handled. This bounds silent worker death (wedged process, externally killed window) to the next inbound event. It does NOT weaken the "NEVER poll windows-list while idle" rule above: this reconcile fires ONLY on an inbound message — event-triggered, never a timer, never an idle loop.

Stop-hook idle notifications of the form "Idling — awaiting supervisor task payload" / "Idling for supervisor acceptance or pushback" / "Report delivered to supervisor. Idling for accept or pushback" are EXPECTED lifecycle output from the /tmux:execute skill and DO NOT count as untagged-output violations. They mean the worker has finished its turn and is waiting. Do not REMIND, do not advance state — simply wait for the next real message.

branch.action: Answer with windows-message to that wid, drawing from (a) the task's SCOPE/CONTEXT, (b) prior accepted reports in research-root, (c) files on disk. If the NEED_INPUT presents RESOLVABLE options (a technical/architectural fork), auto-resolve per the Decision-resolution policy (quality-in-scope, see <autonomy-policy>): answer with the highest-quality option that does NOT expand the goal's scope, plus a one-line rationale — do NOT bounce it to the user. If still unanswerable, reply [SUPERVISOR:NARROW-SCOPE] with a reduced problem statement the worker CAN answer, rather than escalating. Escalate to user ONLY for user-only knowledge (credentials, intent, out-of-band facts) that no file-reading can supply.
Proceed to step 6 — Read the cited file and verify against DELIVERABLE + pushback catalogue.

Read REASON. First attempt: kill and respawn with narrowed scope. If a second respawn on the same subtask class FAILs with the same reason, escalate to user with the failure chain + a proposed pivot. NEVER silently retry the same prompt.

branch.note: The worker is blocked by a prerequisite OUT of this goal's scope. The fix is to BUILD a prerequisite goal first (re-planning / generation), NOT to re-dispatch the implementer. The supervisor cannot create that goal (goal-create races the daemon on goals.yaml) and cannot itself route to generation (a SupervisorSignal's status is never routed — only a validator's ValidatorSignal reaches bounceToGeneration). So the supervisor records a goal-scoped escalation marker that the validator (validate.xml / investigate.xml) consumes into a spec-defect finding next cycle. The supervisor's job here is: validate the request, write/append the marker (capped), suppress the code-retry, and finish the cycle normally.

check.condition: need= is empty, missing, or contentless (e.g. ''/'none'/'n/a'/'tbd'/'fix it'/a vague gripe with no buildable prerequisite)
check.action: Reply via windows-message to that wid: [SUPERVISOR:REMIND] ESCALATE needs a concrete, buildable prerequisite in need= (e.g. need="JWT auth middleware goal: validate Bearer token, reject 401"). Re-send [EXECUTE:ESCALATE ...] with a buildable need=, or use [EXECUTE:FAILED] if the blocker is actually in-scope.
Do NOT relay, do NOT write escalation.md, do NOT advance state. Wait for the worker's next message.

check.condition: GOAL_MODE is false (no active goal)
check.action: There is no goal to escalate and no generation phase to route to. Treat as a terminal FAILED: handle per the [EXECUTE:FAILED ...] branch (narrowed respawn / user escalation). Log that ESCALATE was downgraded to FAILED because the session is standalone.

check.condition: need= is concrete AND GOAL_MODE is true
check.action: Read .tmux-cli/goals/<GOAL_ID>/escalation.md if it exists; parse escalation_count (default 0 if the file or field is absent). Let N = escalation_count.

check.condition: N >= 2 (cap reached — recommended cap is 2, ≤ MaxSpecRetries; see <escalation-routing>)
check.action: Do NOT relay a 3rd escalation (loop guard against endless re-planning). Log: "escalation cap (2) reached for <GOAL_ID> — not relaying; routing to normal terminal path." Handle this worker per the [EXECUTE:FAILED ...] branch instead (narrowed respawn / user escalation). The daemon's SpecRetries backstop (bounceToGeneration hard-halts at 0) remains the authoritative ceiling.

Write/append to .tmux-cli/goals/<GOAL_ID>/escalation.md using the schema in <escalation-routing>: set escalation_count = N+1 and append one entry block carrying need=, the worker wid, and the current cycle. Create the file (with the "## Escalations" header) if absent; otherwise update escalation_count in place and append the entry — never clobber prior entries. This append ALWAYS happens and the entry counts toward the cap 2 whether the prerequisite is later resolved in-run or relayed to generation.

TRIAGE need= by goal.md's size threshold (reuse the SAME size vocabulary as goal.md / the cap language — do not invent a new heuristic): SMALL = an implied change of < ~10 files with no new subsystem/bounded-context and no own planning/research; anything above (≥ ~10 files, a new subsystem/bounded-context, or its own planning/research) is GOAL-SIZED. In-run resolution is the DEFAULT for a SMALL need; a GOAL-SIZED need takes today's generation relay directly.

check.condition: need= is SMALL — in-run resolution (the new default)
check.action: Add the continuation line `status: in-run-attempt` to the just-appended block (immediately after its `- need:`/`from:` lines, before the next `- need:` entry or EOF — a same-block continuation the daemon's parseEscalationMd and investigate.xml's step-2e filter read byte-for-byte).

Spawn ONE narrow prerequisite worker via windows-spawn-worker (the ONLY spawn primitive — never windows-create + windows-send) with: supervisorWid=SUPERVISOR_WID; prefix="prereq-" (the returned wid takes the form prereq-<GID>-<n>); subtask=a one-line label of the need; contextFile=a short context .md you first write under research-root capturing the need + its verification command; scope = the need= text PLUS its verification command PLUS "commit your own change" PLUS the hard rule "you may NEVER [EXECUTE:ESCALATE] — one level only; for ANY blocker emit [EXECUTE:FAILED] instead"; and run it in the SAME goal checkout as this goal's other workers by mirroring step 4's spawn-cwd convention verbatim, so the prereq commit lands in the isolated goal tree and merges back. Do NOT kill the blocked ESCALATE-ing worker yet — it idles for [SUPERVISOR:PREREQ-DONE].
Idle for the prereq worker's reply (event-triggered per the step-5 idle rules — never a sleep/poll).

check.condition: prereq worker replies [EXECUTE:DONE] AND committed a real commit <sha>
check.action: Rewrite that block's `status: in-run-attempt` line to `resolved: in-run (cycle <C>, commit <sha>)` (same-block continuation) — this marks the entry HANDLED so investigate.xml step 2e does NOT relay it and the verdict is not forced to blocked.
windows-kill the prereq worker by NAME.

Send [SUPERVISOR:PREREQ-DONE need=<need> commit=<sha>] via windows-message to the blocked worker. This resume is UNCHARGED — do NOT decrement a code-retry and do NOT charge a SpecRetry. The worker re-checks the blocker and resumes; step 5 keeps idling for its eventual [EXECUTE:DONE]/[EXECUTE:FAILED].
Do NOT fall through to the relay fallback below — the prerequisite was resolved in-run.

check.condition: prereq worker replies [EXECUTE:FAILED], dies, or reports DONE with NO commit sha
check.action: STRIP the `status: in-run-attempt` line from that block, leaving a bare `- need:` entry (the entry still counts toward the cap 2). windows-kill the prereq worker if still alive. Then FALL THROUGH to the relay fallback below — today's behaviour VERBATIM.

check.condition: need= is GOAL-SIZED (relay directly, no in-run attempt) OR the in-run attempt above failed/was stripped — the relay fallback (today's behaviour verbatim)
check.action: SUPPRESS the code-retry: do NOT respawn this worker's scope (re-dispatching the implementer against an unwinnable spec is exactly what ESCALATE avoids). Mark the task done/escalated in tasks-path.
windows-kill the worker by NAME (e.g. "execute-7").

Do NOT write any signal.json here, and NEVER write a source="validator" signal during the supervising phase — checkSupervisingPhase type-asserts *SupervisorSignal and treats any other type as a code-defect failed cycle (the opposite of intent). The supervisor only writes its ordinary SupervisorSignal at cycle completion (step 9b). The escalation reaches generation via the validator relay, not via any supervisor-written signal.

Finish the cycle normally (continue to step 7 / step 9b). Next validation cycle, validate.xml / investigate.xml reads escalation.md and emits the blocked+planner spec-defect finding that the daemon routes to bounceToGeneration (SpecRetries−1).

branch.tag: worker emitted FINDINGS/RISKS/etc. inline in tmux without a file= tag
branch.action: This violates the file-only response protocol. Reply via windows-message with: [SUPERVISOR:REMIND] Use the file= response protocol. Save to {research-root}/execute-<N>-<slug>.md (research-root resolved in step 0b) and reply with only [EXECUTE:DONE file=<abs-path>].
Do not advance state; wait for the file-form reply.

branch.tag: no [EXECUTE:...] header at all AND not a stop-hook idle line
branch.action: Reply ONCE via windows-message with: REMIND: respond only with [EXECUTE:NEED_INPUT|DONE|FAILED] headers (DONE must include file=<abs-path>).
Do not advance state for that worker.

step.title: Continuous effectivity check (autonomous)
step.condition: An [EXECUTE:DONE] has been accepted in step 6, AND other workers are still running or queued.
step.action: Before moving on, re-evaluate every running and queued worker against the new finding: - REFUTED: if the new finding demotes the running worker's premise or marks its scope as now-pointless → send [SUPERVISOR:KILL] via windows-message (worker exits cleanly) OR windows-kill the window directly; remove the task entry from tasks-path and log the reason (leave wid gaps — never renumber). Do not let the worker burn budget on a now-pointless scope. - NARROWED: if the new finding identifies a specific symbol or constraint that tightens the running worker's focus → send [SUPERVISOR:AMEND] with the narrowed scope; worker applies it mid-run. - PREREQUISITE-UNBLOCKED: if the new finding is the prerequisite a queued dependent was waiting for → spawn the dependent now in parallel with remaining running workers; do not wait. - NEW-SURFACE: if the new finding exposes a high-yield parallel task → spawn it immediately alongside existing workers per <ranking>; do not wait for current workers to finish first.
step.when: Fire this check after EVERY accepted DONE (and after every FAILED with pivot). Do not skip. It is the mechanism that keeps the worker pool effective, not just busy.
step.autonomy-ref: Apply per <autonomy-policy> — decisions here are not user-gated.

step.title: Verify DONE against the file — pushback catalogue
step.action: Read the .md at the file= path cited in the DONE tag. If the file does not exist, reply [SUPERVISOR:REMIND] file= path does not exist — resave and re-tag, and wait.
step.rule: Check the file contents against these gaps. Pushback at most 3x per worker. Each pushback MUST cite exactly one gap.
step.gap: a FILES path in the report is missing / unreadable / off-scope
FINDINGS lack file:line or symbol references where code-level detail was required
RISKS is "none" when SCOPE demanded risk analysis
RECOMMENDATION contradicts FINDINGS or restates the task without a decision verb (use|avoid|rewrite|keep|split|merge)
a DELIVERABLE field is empty or single-line when multi-line was demanded
step.action: Pushback format: windows-message to that wid with [EXECUTE:PUSHBACK n=<1|2|3> gap=<G1..G5>]\n<one-sentence specific ask tied to the gap>\nUpdate the same .md file, then re-tag [EXECUTE:DONE file=<same-path>].

check.condition: file contents pass all gap checks
check.action: Accept. Proceed to step 7.

check.condition: 3 pushbacks already sent to this worker
check.action: Accept the last DONE as-is. Log the unresolved gap in your user-facing summary. Proceed to step 7.

step.title: Kill window and update task state
step.action: Mark the corresponding task as status=done in tasks-path.
Call windows-kill with windowId = the NAME (e.g. "execute-7"), not the @id
step.critical: If windows-kill returns "invalid window ID format: window IDs not allowed", you passed @N — retry with the name

step.title: Amend-protocol for in-flight workers (optional)
step.condition: User changes scope or protocol mid-session while a worker is still running
step.action: Send via windows-message a [SUPERVISOR:AMEND] or [SUPERVISOR:AMEND-PROTOCOL] block explaining the new instruction. The worker will apply it to the current task.
step.example: New save-path rule, new response format, additional scope item.

step.title: Findings-driven replan (autonomous)
step.condition: An accepted worker's RECOMMENDATION materially changes the queued plan — demotes a primary surface, refutes a downstream worker's premise, adds a prerequisite, or identifies a queued worker as now-pointless.
step.action: Update the queue AUTONOMOUSLY: - If a queued worker is now pointless, remove its task entry from tasks-path + log reason in synthesis. - If a queued worker now needs narrower/broader scope, rewrite its task-message template — do not ask. - If a new follow-up emerged and fits budget, insert at the correct dependency position per <ranking>.
step.autonomy-ref: Apply per <autonomy-policy> — replan is not user-gated.
step.report: In the step-9 synthesis, list every queue change (added / killed / rewritten) with the finding that drove it. This is the user-facing receipt, not a gate.

step.title: Synthesize (does NOT terminate the session)
step.action: Merge all accepted file= contents into a single user-facing answer. State overlaps and conflicts explicitly. Keep it tight.

For long-running sessions, consider writing a session-synthesis .md (e.g. NN_session-<topic>.md) into research-root that cites each per-worker file and captures the consolidated state. The user often asks for this at the end anyway.

Writing synthesis does NOT return control to the user. After synthesis, re-run §8a against the synthesis's OWN "proposed next wave" / "queue changes" / "follow-ups" sections. If any listed workers are executable (scope fully self-contained — no user-only authorization required), CLASSIFY the wave first: if its tasks trace back to requirements in the original $ARGUMENTS input, it is NOT self-generated — loop freely; if its tasks originate solely from the supervisor's own synthesis/§8a output, increment SELF_WAVE. If SELF_WAVE would exceed 2, do NOT spawn — list the remaining self-generated follow-ups in the synthesis as RECOMMENDED NEXT WORK (not executed) and stop per <stop-conditions>. Otherwise LOOP BACK to §3 (fan-out decision) and spawn in parallel. Return to the user ONLY when the synthesis's replan produces zero new executable workers AND a real <stop-conditions> rule fires (the SELF_WAVE cap is such a rule). The flow is a LOOP (3→4→5→6→7→[5a]→[8a]→9→[9b]→3), not a one-pass — but the loop is BOUNDED: it must not perpetually generate work from its own output.

step.title: Cycle completion and continuation
step.action: Read tasks-path and mark all completed tasks as status=done.

Append/update a research-file index in .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/goal.md so the goal records its artifacts. Use a section titled "## Research Files" with markdown links RELATIVE to the goal dir, grouped by category:
## Research Files
### Reports - [<subtask label>](research/execute-<N>-<slug>.md) - ... one per accepted per-worker report file ...
### Context - [<subtask label>](research/task-<slug>.md) - ... one per research-task context file ...
If a "## Research Files" section already exists in goal.md (re-run / next cycle), REPLACE that section in place rather than appending a duplicate (idempotent). List every generated file in its category. Omit a subsection if it has no files.
Read supervisor.max_cycles from .tmux-cli/setting.yaml (default: 0 = unlimited).
Increment the cycle counter in tasks-path.

check.condition: pending tasks remain AND (max_cycles == 0 OR cycle < max_cycles)
check.action: Send /clear to SUPERVISOR_WID via windows-send.

Send /tmux:supervisor {tasks-path} to SUPERVISOR_WID via windows-send (the resolved tasks-path — goal-scoped in GOAL_MODE, top-level otherwise). Step 1c's continuation check matches this same tasks-path argument.
STOP — a fresh supervisor instance takes over with the updated tasks-path as input.

check.condition: max_cycles reached (cycle >= max_cycles AND max_cycles > 0)
check.action: Print to user: "Cycle limit reached ({cycle}/{max_cycles}). Remaining pending tasks: [list task names]."

If GOAL_MODE is true, REUSE the GOAL_ID already resolved in step 0b — do NOT re-read the global current-goal marker at write time (the daemon rewrites it on every dispatch; under concurrent dispatch it is last-writer-wins and may name ANOTHER goal, misrouting this signal into the wrong goal's folder and corrupting daemon goal-completion state). Write .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/signal.json with {"source":"supervisor","status":"stopped","reason":"max_cycles_reached"}.
STOP.

check.condition: no pending tasks remain in tasks-path BUT the §8a/§9 replan produced a NEXT WAVE that is executable (SELF_WAVE rules applied exactly as in step 9) and that wave exists as a PLAN DOCUMENT rather than as pending tasks-path entries
check.order: evaluated BEFORE the no-pending-tasks normal stop below
check.action:
Write the next wave out as a plan document at {research-root}/next-wave-<W>.md, where <W> is this wave's FILENAME index: the SMALLEST positive integer for which {research-root}/next-wave-<W>.md does NOT already exist. Never overwrite an existing next-wave file. (Do NOT use SELF_WAVE as the index: a wave traceable to the original $ARGUMENTS does not increment it, so two consecutive non-self-generated waves would collide on one filename and the second write would destroy the first's still-unconsumed plan.) <W> is an identifier only — it is never a counter and never what the fresh instance adopts; the frontmatter below is the SOLE counter source. Lead the file with a YAML frontmatter block carrying the counters the fresh instance must resume:
--- self_wave: <post-increment SELF_WAVE> cycle: <current cycle counter> ---
Below the frontmatter, write the wave as normal free-form plan prose the supervisor can self-spec and fan out from (§3). Step 0c of the fresh instance reads exactly this frontmatter to adopt the counters.
check.rule: The SELF_WAVE cap and max_cycles are enforced HERE, before anything is written: if the wave is self-generated and the post-increment SELF_WAVE would exceed 2, or max_cycles is reached (cycle >= max_cycles AND max_cycles > 0), do NOT write the plan file and do NOT arm a handoff — fall through to the normal stop and list the wave in the synthesis as RECOMMENDED NEXT WORK per <stop-conditions>. A /clear must never launder a cap.

branch.action:
Follow the /tmux:supervisor:fresh procedure: write the one-shot handoff marker .tmux-cli/fresh-handoff ATOMICALLY (write a temp file, then rename over the target) as the LAST action before ending the turn, with these fields:
plan: {research-root}/next-wave-<W>.md self_wave: <post-increment SELF_WAVE> cycle: <current cycle counter> requested_by: supervisor created: <current UTC timestamp, RFC3339>

Print EXACTLY one line, byte-identical in shape to the /tmux:supervisor:fresh confirmation (fresh.xml step 5) so one grep matches both surfaces: "FRESH HANDOFF armed → {research-root}/next-wave-<W>.md (self_wave=<N>, cycle=<M>)" — where <N> is the post-increment SELF_WAVE and <M> the cycle counter just written to the marker. The arrow is a literal U+2192 (→), never an HTML entity.

STOP — end the turn here. Do NOT windows-send /clear and do NOT windows-send /tmux:supervisor for this branch. The tmux-supervisor-cycle.sh Stop hook fires at turn end, consumes the marker, and performs the /clear + /tmux:supervisor {plan} relaunch deterministically from OUTSIDE this dying context. Sending them yourself would double-fire the restart.

Keep the existing windows-send dance — the marker path is NOT available here: the Stop hook defers whenever .tmux-cli/taskvisor-active exists (the daemon is the sole dispatcher in goal mode), so a marker written in GOAL_MODE would never be consumed and would dead-end. Send /clear to SUPERVISOR_WID via windows-send, then send /tmux:supervisor {research-root}/next-wave-<W>.md to SUPERVISOR_WID via windows-send.
STOP — a fresh supervisor instance takes over with the next-wave plan file as input.

check.condition: no pending tasks remain AND the next-wave plan-file branch above did NOT fire (no executable next wave, or it was capped)
check.action: Normal stop — all work is complete.

If GOAL_MODE is true, REUSE the GOAL_ID already resolved in step 0b — same rule as the max_cycles branch above: never re-read the global current-goal marker for a signal write (last-writer-wins under concurrent dispatch). Write .tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/signal.json with {"source":"supervisor","status":"done"}.

task-message-template.note: REFERENCE ONLY — this template is assembled server-side by windows-spawn-worker. You do NOT need to format or send it manually. Shown here for documentation of what the worker receives.
task-message-template.template:
SUPERVISOR_WID=<your SUPERVISOR_WID name> SELF_WID=<the execute-N name> SUBTASK: <one-line label>
SCOPE: Full spec is in <context-file-path> — read it and follow it.
<multi-line scope summary — files, directories, what to implement or investigate>
CONTEXT: <multi-line supporting context — prior findings, constraints, non-goals>
DELIVERABLE (write these sections INSIDE the report .md; do NOT paste them into tmux): - FINDINGS: >=3 bullets, each with file path or symbol reference - RISKS: bullets OR the literal word "none" - RECOMMENDATION: 1–3 sentences, must contain a verb of decision (use|avoid|rewrite|keep|split|merge) - FILES: absolute paths touched or cited, one per line
RESPONSE PROTOCOL (MANDATORY): 1. Save the full report to {research-root}/execute-<N>-<slug>.md (research-root resolved in step 0b) using the sections specified in DELIVERABLE above (or ## FINDINGS / ## RISKS / ## RECOMMENDATION / ## FILES when no custom deliverable is set). Pick a descriptive <slug> (kebab-case, ≤40 chars). 2. Reply via windows-message to SUPERVISOR_WID with ONLY: [EXECUTE:DONE wid=<self> sup=<supervisor> file=<abs-path-to-your-md>] Do NOT inline report content in the tmux message. The file IS the report. 3. [EXECUTE:NEED_INPUT wid=<self> sup=<supervisor> ...] and [EXECUTE:FAILED wid=<self> sup=<supervisor> reason=...] may carry a short (<200 char) inline reason. 4. If the supervisor sends [EXECUTE:PUSHBACK ...], update the SAME .md file and re-tag [EXECUTE:DONE wid=<self> sup=<supervisor> file=<same-path>].

execution-rules.rule: windows-spawn-worker: single atomic call to spawn a worker (creates window, sends /tmux:execute, sends task message with DELIVERABLE + RESPONSE PROTOCOL)
NEVER assemble spawn steps manually — always use windows-spawn-worker
windows-message: all subsequent supervisor→worker communication (answers, pushbacks, reminders, amends)
windows-kill: only on accepted DONE or terminal FAILED; never on SUPERVISOR_WID; pass window NAME
Always Read the file cited in a DONE before applying the gap catalogue; never rely on the (absent) inline body
If a worker sends DONE without file=, REMIND once; do not accept an inline-body DONE as a shortcut

windows-send: used ONLY for self-directed cycle-restart commands to SUPERVISOR_WID in step 9b (/clear, /tmux:supervisor). Two step-9b restart paths, kept distinct: (a) the NEXT-WAVE PLAN-FILE restart uses windows-send in GOAL_MODE only; standalone cycle/wave restarts go through the fresh-handoff marker (.tmux-cli/fresh-handoff, consumed by the tmux-supervisor-cycle.sh Stop hook) — the supervisor writes the marker and STOPs, it does NOT send. (b) the TASKS-PATH continuation (pending tasks remain) still uses windows-send in BOTH modes today — migrating it onto the marker is a listed fast-follow, so do not remove those sends.
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.

stop-conditions.rule: STOP and return user-facing synthesis ONLY when one of: - All queued workers completed AND findings-driven replan (step 8a) produced no new executable workers. - 2 consecutive FAILEDs on the same subtask class after narrowing. - Headline finding requiring user-only authorization (credentials, out-of-band intent, scope change beyond project). - Token/time budget exhausted (if user set one). - Cycle limit reached (supervisor.max_cycles in .tmux-cli/setting.yaml; step 9b). - SELF_WAVE cap reached (2 self-generated waves per CHAIN — across every fresh-handoff /clear, not merely per context; see glossary): the next wave's tasks originate solely from the supervisor's own synthesis/§8a output and SELF_WAVE is already 2 — list the remaining self-generated follow-ups in the synthesis as RECOMMENDED NEXT WORK (not executed) and stop. Waves traceable to the original $ARGUMENTS requirements are NOT capped. - STOP GATE triggered (step 1c) — unfinished tasks from a previous run. - Explicit user interrupt.
stop-conditions.not-stop: These are NOT stop conditions — resolve autonomously per <autonomy-policy>: - "I have two reasonable options" → pick per <ranking>. - "Queued worker scope needs adjustment" → adjust and continue. - "Worker recommended a new follow-up" → add and continue. - "Worker finding contradicts a prior worker" → record; most-recent-with-most-evidence is truth-of-record. - "I just wrote a session-synthesis .md" → synthesis does not terminate the session; re-run §8a against its own contents and loop to §3 if new workers are executable AND the SELF_WAVE cap (2 self-generated waves) is not yet reached — at the cap, self-generated follow-ups become RECOMMENDED NEXT WORK instead. - "A plan document I wrote says Stage N after Stage M closes" / "wave N+1 after wave N" → self-imposed organizational gate; NOT user-imposed authorization. Continue through stages. - "A recommended wave-N+1 set exists in synthesis" → those are §8a replan items; spawn them in parallel per <ranking> unless stop-conditions actually fire — the SELF_WAVE cap IS such a stop-condition: purely self-generated waves beyond 2 are listed as RECOMMENDED NEXT WORK, not spawned.

llm.critical-rules: • DO NOT use sleep; idle is the trigger • DO NOT kill your own SUPERVISOR_WID • DO NOT treat stop-hook idle notifications as untagged-output violations — they are expected lifecycle • DO NOT advance state on untagged non-idle output — reply once with REMIND and keep waiting • DO NOT pass @N to windows-kill — use the window NAME • DO NOT accept DONE without file=<abs-path> — REMIND to use file-only protocol • DO NOT spawn any worker (step 4) before tasks.yaml is written and verified (step 3b) — this is a blocking gate, no exceptions • DO NOT violate <autonomy-policy> — never ask the user outside the two defined exits • DO NOT default to serial — default to maximum parallelism; serialize only when later worker's scope is literally unconstructable without prior worker's output • DO NOT let a running worker finish on a now-pointless scope — amend or kill via [SUPERVISOR:AMEND]/[SUPERVISOR:KILL] when new findings refute its premise (step 5a) • DO NOT skip the step-5a effectivity check after each DONE — it is the mechanism that keeps the pool effective, not just busy • DO NOT treat self-written plan documents (stage gates, "wave 6 after wave 5", "Stage 2 after Stage 1", "deferred to next session", numbered-wave boundaries) as user-imposed pauses — they are organizational, not authorization boundaries; continue per <autonomy-policy> • DO NOT stop after writing a session-synthesis .md — re-run §8a against the synthesis itself; if it names executable follow-up workers in "proposed next wave" / "queue changes", spawn them per §3 fan-out, do not return to the user — UNLESS the SELF_WAVE cap (2 self-generated waves per invocation) is reached: then list them as RECOMMENDED NEXT WORK and stop per <stop-conditions> • SOLE solo-lane carve-out (data-driven only): when the upstream-decided lane resolves to solo (resolution order: tasks-path top-level `lane` key → goal.md `## Lane` → full) AND exactly 1 task is pending in tasks-path, step 3c executes that task inline in this window with zero spawns — perceived simplicity remains an INVALID activation reason; only the upstream lane value activates this carve-out, never the supervisor's own judgment.
